qa_pairs.question: When will StdEnv/2023 become the default environment for clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: StdEnv/2023 will become the default environment for all clusters after April 1, 2024.

qa_pairs.question: How can I change my default standard environment?
qa_pairs.answer: You can change your default environment by modifying the `$HOME/.modulerc` file, for example, by running `echo "module-version StdEnv/2020 default" >> $HOME/.modulerc`.

qa_pairs.question: What is required for a change to my default standard environment to take effect?
qa_pairs.answer: You must log out and log in again for the change to your default standard environment to take effect.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to recompile my code or reinstall packages when the StdEnv changes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you compile your own code or have installed R or Python packages, you should recompile your code or reinstall the packages you need with the newest version of the standard environment.

qa_pairs.question: How can I continue using an earlier standard environment for an existing workflow?
qa_pairs.answer: To use an earlier environment, simply add `module load StdEnv/2020` (or the specific version) to your job scripts before loading any other modules.

qa_pairs.question: Will earlier standard environments be removed?
qa_pairs.answer: No, earlier environments and their dependent software will remain available, but versions 2016.4 and 2018.3 are no longer supported, and staff will only install software in StdEnv/2023.

qa_pairs.question: Can I mix modules from different standard environments within a single job?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should use a single environment for a given job, as mixing different environments can lead to unpredictable results and errors.

qa_pairs.question: Which standard environment should I use for new projects or to access newer applications?
qa_pairs.answer: You should use StdEnv/2023 for new projects or to use newer versions of applications by adding `module load StdEnv/2023` to your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to load modules in my .bashrc file for using an older environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, loading modules in your `.bashrc` is not recommended; instead, you should explicitly load modules in your job scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Does this standard environment change affect users who only use cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this change only affects available software accessed by using environment modules on HPC clusters, not cloud resources.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if a module I previously used is no longer loadable?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run the `module avail` command, for example, `module avail gcc`, to see the available versions, as more recent versions of most applications are installed in the new environment.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about the differences between StdEnv/2023 and earlier environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The differences between StdEnv/2023 and earlier environments are discussed in "Standard software environments."
